content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

January 17, 1977


Karl A. Cozad
Yuba County Clerk and Recorder
Courthouse
Marysville, CA 95901

Dear Mr. Cozad:

     Thank you for your letter of January 7, 1977, in which you inquire about the appropriate steps to take when you suspect that there has been a failure to disclose required information on a Statement of Economic Interests filed with your office.

     Government Code Section 81010(d) states that a filing officer shall "report apparent violations of [the Political Reform Act] to the appropriate agencies . . ." Accordingly, if you believe that the individuals mentioned in your letter have failed to properly complete their Statements of Economic Interests, or have failed to disqualify themselves from participating in governmental decisions in which they had a financial interest, you should contact the District Attorney since he is given enforcement authority for such violations. See Government Code Section 91001.

     Thank you for your interest in the Political Reform Act. If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth Finney
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

KF:jo


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 222-6441

Mrs. Donna Seaman
Senior Deputy County Clerk
743-1511 Ext. 42

Miss Margaret Luyster
Senior Recorder's Clerk
743-1511 Ext. 55

F.P.P.
JAN 11 9.92 M 77

Mrs. Patricia Stewart
Boards & Commissions Secretary
743-1511 Ext. 81

Mrs. Mary Reynolds
Elections Clerk
743-1511 Ext. 42

KARL A. COZAD
COUNTY CLERK & RECORDER
COUNTY OF YUBA
Courthouse
Marysville, California 95901

January 7, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
P.O.Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

On January 3, 1977, the Directors of the Olivehurst Public
Utility District filed Statements of Economic Interest with
this office. In reviewing the Statements, I noted that two
of the Directors may have potential conflict of interests in
the operation of the O.P.U.D. business affairs and the
employment of each of the directors.

The possible conflicts are as follows:

1. Director Francis David Becker is employed full time
by his father doing business as Becker's Auto Supply
Company. The Olivehurst Public Utility District
purchases tires from Becker's Auto Supply for the
various vehicles owned and operated by the District.

2. Director Otis Dale Browning is employed full time
as General Foreman and Chief Estimator for W. V.
Alton Sheet Metal Company. W. V. Alton was the
successful bidder as a sub-contractor to perform
sheetmetal and heating work amounting to several
thousands of dollars on a new sewage treatment plant
being constructed by O.P.U.D. Mr. Browning worked
directly on and supervised the construction phase of
the project.

Page 2
January 7, 1977


Neither director indicated on his report or by any other
document whether or not he has disqualified himself from
participating in the legislative process regarding either
possible conflict.

The Government Code Sections regarding Conflict of Interest
are somewhat nebulous as to my duties when possible conflict
of interests arise with legislators in the County.

Please aswise me what action, if any, I should take as Yuba
County Clerk.

Sincerely,

KARL A. COZAD
Yuba County Clerk
and Recorder

In reviewing the Statements, I noted that two of the Directors may have potential conflict of interests in the operation of the O.P.U.D. business affairs and the employment of each of the directors.
